nodes.name: 1. Webhook: Query Graph
nodes.notes: ENTRY POINT: Receives Cypher queries from other agents. POST body: { query: 'MATCH ...', mode: 'read|write' }

nodes.name: 2. Validate & Prepare Query
nodes.notes: SECURITY: Validates that read-mode queries don't contain write operations. Prevents accidental data modification.

nodes.name: 3. Execute via Neo4j HTTP API
nodes.notes: NEO4J CONNECTION: Uses HTTP API at port 7474. Credential needs Neo4j username (neo4j) and password. Alternative: Use MCP Client Tool if running neo4j-mcp server.

nodes.name: 4. Parse & Format Results
nodes.notes: DATA TRANSFORMATION: Converts Neo4j's row-based response into JSON objects with named properties for easier consumption by other agents.

nodes.name: 5. Respond to Caller
nodes.notes: RESPONSE: Returns standardized JSON with success, results array, columns, rowCount, and any errors to the calling agent.
